File: crates/providers/src/openai.rs:789
Author: greptile-apps[bot]
Body: **Double `Done` event emitted on clean `response.completed`**

When `response.completed` is received, `process_responses_sse_line` returns `SseLineResult::Events(events)` with `StreamEvent::Done` embedded in the list (see `openai_compat.rs`). That `Done` is yielded by the loop above. If the HTTP connection then closes without a `[DONE]` sentinel (which is common — many Responses API implementations close the TCP stream directly after `response.completed`), execution falls through to here.

Because `state.input_tokens` was already populated during `response.completed` processing, the condition `state.input_tokens > 0 || state.output_tokens > 0` is `true`, and a **second** `StreamEvent::Done` is emitted.

The existing test `responses_sse_stream_text_delta` silently masks this because it uses `done_usage = Some(u)` (overwrite semantics), so the duplicate event goes undetected.

To fix, track whether `Done` has already been emitted:

```
// Before the byte-stream loop:
let mut done_emitted = false;
```

Then inside the `SseLineResult::Events` arm, set `done_emitted = true` if any of the yielded events is `StreamEvent::Done`. Finally guard the EOF block:

```rust
if !done_emitted && (state.input_tokens > 0 || state.output_tokens > 0) {
    yield StreamEvent::Done(Usage {
        input_tokens: state.input_tokens,
        output_tokens: state.output_tokens,
        ..Default::default()
    });
}
```
In-reply-to: none
ID: 2901787451

---
File: crates/providers/src/openai_compat.rs:1009
Author: greptile-apps[bot]
Body: **`process_responses_sse_line` never emits `ProviderRaw` events**

`process_openai_sse_line` (line 764) unconditionally prepends `StreamEvent::ProviderRaw(evt.clone())` to every event batch:

```rust
let mut events = vec![StreamEvent::ProviderRaw(evt.clone())];
```

`process_responses_sse_line` has no equivalent — raw Responses API frames are never surfaced as `ProviderRaw`. Any upstream consumer (telemetry, replay tooling, UI debugging panels) that relies on `ProviderRaw` to inspect raw provider traffic will silently receive no events for the Responses API SSE path.

Consider adding `StreamEvent::ProviderRaw(evt.clone())` at the top of `process_responses_sse_line`, mirroring the Chat Completions path.
In-reply-to: none
ID: 2901787461

--- Author: greptile-apps[bot] Body:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds first-class OpenAI Responses API support alongside the existing Chat Completions path. It introduces a new `WireApi` config enum (`chat-completions` | `responses`), plumbs it through `ProviderEntry` and `OpenAiProvider`, and adds a dedicated SSE streaming path (`stream_responses_sse`) plus shared helpers (`process_responses_sse_line`, `to_responses_api_tools`, `to_responses_input`) in `openai_compat.rs`. The WebSocket path already used Responses-format payloads; the SSE and non-streaming `complete` paths are now also branched on `wire_api`. **Key findings:** - **Double `Done` event in `stream_responses_sse`** — `process_responses_sse_line` returns `SseLineResult::Events` (not `SseLineResult::Done`) for `response.completed`, embedding `StreamEvent::Done` in the event list. After the stream closes without a `[DONE]` sentinel, the EOF fallback at the end of `stream_responses_sse` emits a second `Done` because `state.input_tokens > 0`. The existing test silently masks this by overwriting `done_usage` rather than asserting an exact count. - **Missing `ProviderRaw` events** — `process_responses_sse_line` never emits `StreamEvent::ProviderRaw`, unlike `process_openai_sse_line`, creating an observability gap for consumers that rely on raw event inspection. - **Inconsistent `/v1` URL normalization** — `responses_websocket_url` always ensures `/v1` is present in the path; `responses_sse_url` does not, meaning the same `base_url` config value can resolve to different endpoints depending on the transport chosen.

Confidence Score: 3/5

- Safe to merge after fixing the double-Done emission bug in stream_responses_sse; config and compat changes are clean. - The config layer, validation, and URL-building code are minimal and correct. The double-Done bug in stream_responses_sse is a real logic error that will emit two StreamEvent::Done events whenever response.completed arrives and the stream closes without a trailing [DONE] sentinel — a common real-world scenario. While this may not crash callers today (many likely treat the first Done as canonical), it is incorrect contract behaviour and could break stricter consumers. The missing ProviderRaw events and URL-normalization inconsistency are lower severity but worth addressing. Validation was explicitly skipped per the PR description, increasing uncertainty. - crates/providers/src/openai.rs (double-Done bug in stream_responses_sse, inconsistent URL normalization) and crates/providers/src/openai_compat.rs (missing ProviderRaw events in process_responses_sse_line).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | crates/providers/src/openai.rs | Adds `wire_api: WireApi` field and SSE/dispatch logic for the Responses API. Contains a double-Done emission bug in `stream_responses_sse` when the stream closes without `[DONE]`, and inconsistent URL normalization vs the WebSocket path. | | crates/providers/src/openai_compat.rs | Adds `process_responses_sse_line`, `ResponsesStreamState`, `to_responses_api_tools`, and `to_responses_input`. Logic is sound; notable gap is the absence of `ProviderRaw` events (unlike the Chat Completions path). | | crates/config/src/schema.rs | Adds `WireApi` enum and `wire_api` field on `ProviderEntry` with correct serde defaults and skip-serialization-if guard. Clean and well-structured. | | crates/config/src/validate.rs | Adds `wire_api` to the static schema map for unknown-field validation. Minimal, correct change. | | crates/config/src/lib.rs | Re-exports `WireApi` from the config crate public API. Trivial, correct change. | | crates/providers/src/lib.rs | Wires `entry.wire_api` through to `OpenAiProvider::with_wire_api` during provider construction. Correctly guarded behind a non-default check. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Caller participant OpenAiProvider participant ResponsesSSE as "Responses SSE /responses" participant ChatSSE as "Chat Completions /chat/completions" participant WebSocket as "WebSocket /v1/responses" Caller->>OpenAiProvider: stream_with_tools(messages, tools) alt wire_api=Responses + transport=SSE OpenAiProvider->>ResponsesSSE: POST /responses (input, stream:true) ResponsesSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: response.output_text.delta -> Delta ResponsesSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: response.output_item.added -> ToolCallStart ResponsesSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: response.function_call_arguments.delta -> ToolCallArgumentsDelta ResponsesSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: response.function_call_arguments.done -> ToolCallComplete ResponsesSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: response.completed -> Done (bug: double-Done at EOF) ResponsesSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: "[DONE]" -> return else wire_api=Responses + transport=WebSocket/Auto OpenAiProvider->>WebSocket: WS connect wss://... OpenAiProvider->>WebSocket: response.create event WebSocket-->>OpenAiProvider: response.output_text.delta -> Delta WebSocket-->>OpenAiProvider: response.completed -> break + Done else wire_api=ChatCompletions + transport=SSE OpenAiProvider->>ChatSSE: POST /chat/completions (messages, stream:true) ChatSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: choices delta -> Delta ChatSSE-->>OpenAiProvider: "[DONE]" -> finalize_stream -> Done end OpenAiProvider-->>Caller: StreamEvent sequence ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `crates/providers/src/openai.rs`, line 621-637 ([link](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/blob/f35b51ba4f091dd7b6fcf2fa2d97a9a26b322904/crates/providers/src/openai.rs#L621-L637)) **Inconsistent `/v1` handling between `responses_sse_url` and `responses_websocket_url`** `responses_websocket_url` (line 621) always ensures `/v1` is appended to the path if not already present: ```rust if !base.ends_with("/v1") { base.push_str("/v1"); } ``` `responses_sse_url` (line 610) does **not** apply the same normalization — it only handles the `/chat/completions` → `/responses` substitution or a bare append. As a result, if a user configures `base_url = "https://api.openai.com"` (without `/v1`), the SSE path resolves to `https://api.openai.com/responses` while the WebSocket path resolves to `wss://api.openai.com/v1/responses`. This inconsistency could cause hard-to-debug 404 errors when switching between transports. Consider either unifying the URL construction into a single helper or documenting the expected format of `base_url` explicitly.
Last reviewed commit: f35b51b --- Author: thomas7725353 Body: All three review findings have been addressed in ca1e5b8e: 1. **Double `Done` event** — Added `done_emitted` flag to `ResponsesStreamState`. The EOF fallback in `stream_responses_sse` now checks `!state.done_emitted` before emitting, preventing the duplicate `Done` when `response.completed` already provided one. Test updated to assert exactly 1 `Done` event. 2. **Missing `ProviderRaw` events** — `process_responses_sse_line` now prepends `StreamEvent::ProviderRaw(evt.clone())` to every event batch, mirroring `process_openai_sse_line`. All branches (including unknown events and empty deltas) emit at least a `ProviderRaw`. 3. **Inconsistent `/v1` URL normalization** — `responses_sse_url` now appends `/v1` for bare base URLs (e.g. `https://api.openai.com` → `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses`), matching `responses_websocket_url` behavior. Added a dedicated test for this case. All tests pass (including 18 `responses_sse_*` tests). --- Author: lijunle Body: I think this could be abandoned after #393 Oh, it actually adds support for `wire_api`.

content.text: Now let me read the openai_compat.rs conflicts.

Now I have a good understanding of all conflicts. Let me resolve them. The key challenge is that main refactored the Responses API code (moved `split_responses_instructions_and_input` to `openai_compat`, added `finalize_responses_stream`, `parse_responses_completion`), while HEAD added the PR fixes (ProviderRaw, double-Done guard, URL normalization).     fn split_responses_instructions_and_input(
        messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
    ) -> (Option<String>, Vec<serde_json::Value>) {
        let mut instruction_parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
        let mut non_system: Vec<ChatMessage> = Vec::new();

        for message in messages {
            match message {
                ChatMessage::System { content } => {
                    if !content.trim().is_empty() {
                        instruction_parts.push(content);
                    }
                },
                other => non_system.push(other),
            }
        }

        let instructions = if instruction_parts.is_empty() {
            None
        } else {
            Some(instruction_parts.join("\n\n"))
        };

        (instructions, to_responses_input(&non_system))
    }

    /// Stream using the OpenAI Responses API format (`/responses`) over SSE.
    #[allow(clippy::collapsible_if)]
    fn stream_responses_sse(
        &self,
        messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
        tools: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
    ) -> Pin<Box<dyn Stream<Item = StreamEvent> + Send + '_>> {
        Box::pin(async_stream::stream! {
            let (instructions, input) = Self::split_responses_instructions_and_input(messages);
            let mut body = serde_json::json!({
                "model": self.model,
                "input": input,
                "stream": true,
            });

            if let Some(instructions) = instructions {
                body["instructions"] = serde_json::Value::String(instructions);
            }

            if !tools.is_empty() {
                body["tools"] = serde_json::Value::Array(to_responses_api_tools(&tools));
                body["tool_choice"] = serde_json::json!("auto");
            }

            debug!(

    async fn start_responses_mock(

                        .await
                        .unwrap_or_default();
                    let body: Option<serde_json::Value> = serde_json::from_slice(&body_bytes).ok();
                    cap.lock().unwrap().push(CapturedRequest { body });

                        .body(axum::body::Body::from(payload))
                        .unwrap()
                }
            }),
        );

            axum::serve(listener, app).await.unwrap();
        });

        (format!("http://{addr}"), captured)

        let mut done_usage = None;
        let mut raw_count = 0u32;
        while let Some(event) = stream.next().await {
            match event {
                StreamEvent::Delta(d) => text.push_str(&d),
                StreamEvent::Done(u) => {
                    done_count += 1;
                    done_usage = Some(u);

        // Must emit ProviderRaw for each SSE event (3 data lines).

// ============================================================================
// OpenAI Responses API SSE streaming

/// State for tracking tool calls during Responses API SSE streaming.
#[derive(Default)]
pub struct ResponsesStreamState {
    /// Next tool call index to assign.
    pub current_tool_index: usize,
    /// Map from output index -> (call_id, name).
    pub tool_calls: HashMap<usize, (String, String)>,
    /// Indices already completed (to avoid duplicate completion events).
    pub completed_tool_calls: std::collections::HashSet<usize>,
    pub input_tokens: u32,
    pub output_tokens: u32,
    /// Whether a `StreamEvent::Done` has already been emitted.
    pub done_emitted: bool,
}

/// Resolve the output index from a Responses API SSE event.
///
/// Tries `output_index`, then `item_index` / `index`, falling back to `fallback`.
fn responses_output_index(event: &serde_json::Value, fallback: usize) -> usize {
    event
        .get("output_index")
        .or_else(|| event.get("item_index"))
        .or_else(|| event.get("index"))
        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64)
        .map(|i| i as usize)
        .unwrap_or(fallback)
}

/// Process a single SSE data line from a Responses API stream.
///
/// Handles the event types emitted by the OpenAI Responses API (`/responses`):
/// - `response.output_text.delta` → `StreamEvent::Delta`
/// - `response.output_item.added` (function_call) → `StreamEvent::ToolCallStart`
/// - `response.function_call_arguments.delta` → `StreamEvent::ToolCallArgumentsDelta`
/// - `response.function_call_arguments.done` → `StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete`
/// - `response.completed` → extract usage → `SseLineResult::Done`
/// - `error` / `response.failed` → `StreamEvent::Error`
pub fn process_responses_sse_line(data: &str, state: &mut ResponsesStreamState) -> SseLineResult {
    if data == "[DONE]" {
        return SseLineResult::Done;
    }

    let Ok(evt) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(data) else {
        return SseLineResult::Skip;
    };

    // Emit ProviderRaw for every parsed event, mirroring the Chat Completions path.
    let raw = StreamEvent::ProviderRaw(evt.clone());

    let evt_type = evt["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("");

    match evt_type {
        "response.output_text.delta" => {
            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
                && !delta.is_empty()
            {
                return SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::Delta(delta.to_string())]);
            }
            SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
        },
        "response.output_item.added" => {
            if evt["item"]["type"].as_str() == Some("function_call") {
                let id = evt["item"]["call_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let name = evt["item"]["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index);
                state.current_tool_index = state.current_tool_index.max(index + 1);
                state.tool_calls.insert(index, (id.clone(), name.clone()));
                return SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::ToolCallStart {
                    id,
                    name,
                    index,
                }]);
            }
            SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
        },
        "response.function_call_arguments.delta" => {
            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
                && !delta.is_empty()
            {
                let index =
                    responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
                return SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::ToolCallArgumentsDelta {
                    index,
                    delta: delta.to_string(),
                }]);
            }
            SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
        },
        "response.function_call_arguments.done" => {
            let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
            if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
                return SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index }]);
            }
            SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
        },
        "response.completed" => {
            let mut events = vec![raw];

            // Extract usage.
            if let Some(usage) = evt.get("response").and_then(|r| r.get("usage")) {
                let parsed = parse_openai_compat_usage(usage);
                state.input_tokens = parsed.input_tokens;
                state.output_tokens = parsed.output_tokens;
            }

            // Complete any remaining tool calls.
            let mut pending: Vec<usize> = state.tool_calls.keys().copied().collect();
            pending.sort_unstable();
            for index in pending {
                if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
                    events.push(StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index });
                }
            }

            state.done_emitted = true;
            events.push(StreamEvent::Done(Usage {
                input_tokens: state.input_tokens,
                output_tokens: state.output_tokens,
                ..Default::default()
            }));
            SseLineResult::Events(events)
        },
        "error" | "response.failed" => {
            let msg = evt["error"]["message"]
                .as_str()
                .or_else(|| evt["response"]["error"]["message"].as_str())
                .or_else(|| evt["message"].as_str())
                .unwrap_or("unknown error");
            SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::Error(msg.to_string())])
        },
        _ => SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw]),
    }
}

// Responses API helpers (shared by openai.rs and github_copilot.rs)
// ============================================================================

/// Split system messages into `instructions` and convert the rest to Responses
/// API `input` items.
///
/// The Responses API uses a top-level `instructions` field instead of a system
/// message role.  This function extracts all system messages, joins them with
/// `\n\n`, and converts the remaining messages via [`to_responses_input`].
#[must_use]
pub fn split_responses_instructions_and_input(
    messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
) -> (Option<String>, Vec<serde_json::Value>) {
    let mut instruction_parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
    let mut non_system: Vec<ChatMessage> = Vec::new();

    for message in messages {
        match message {
            ChatMessage::System { content } => {
                if !content.trim().is_empty() {
                    instruction_parts.push(content);
                }
            },
            other => non_system.push(other),
        }
    }

    let instructions = if instruction_parts.is_empty() {
        None
    } else {
        Some(instruction_parts.join("\n\n"))
    };

    (instructions, to_responses_input(&non_system))
}

/// Resolve the output index from a Responses API event.
///
/// The Responses API uses `output_index` for items and `index` for
/// sub-item fields.  WebSocket events may also use `item_index`.
/// Falls back to `fallback` if none of these keys are present.
pub fn responses_output_index(event: &serde_json::Value, fallback: usize) -> usize {
    event
        .get("output_index")
        .or_else(|| event.get("item_index"))
        .or_else(|| event.get("index"))
        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64)
        .map(|i| i as usize)
        .unwrap_or(fallback)
}

/// State for tracking Responses API SSE streaming.
#[derive(Default)]
pub struct ResponsesStreamState {
    /// Map from index -> (call_id, name)
    pub tool_calls: HashMap<usize, (String, String)>,
    /// Set of tool call indices that have already emitted `ToolCallComplete`.
    pub completed_tool_calls: HashSet<usize>,
    /// The next tool call index to assign.
    pub current_tool_index: usize,
    pub input_tokens: u32,
    pub output_tokens: u32,
    pub cache_read_tokens: u32,
    pub cache_write_tokens: u32,
}

/// Process a single SSE data line from a Responses API stream.
///
/// Returns [`SseLineResult`] indicating whether to skip, yield events, or stop.
///
/// Handles the event types emitted by the Responses API:
/// - `response.output_text.delta` → text delta
/// - `response.output_item.added` (type=function_call) → tool call start
/// - `response.function_call_arguments.delta` → tool call arguments delta
/// - `response.function_call_arguments.done` → tool call complete
/// - `response.completed` → parse usage, done
/// - `error` / `response.failed` → error
pub fn process_responses_sse_line(data: &str, state: &mut ResponsesStreamState) -> SseLineResult {
    if data == "[DONE]" {
        return SseLineResult::Done;
    }

    let Ok(evt) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(data) else {
        return SseLineResult::Skip;
    };

    match evt["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("") {
        "response.output_text.delta" => {
            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
                && !delta.is_empty()
            {
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::Delta(delta.to_string())])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.output_item.added" => {
            if evt["item"]["type"].as_str() == Some("function_call") {
                let id = evt["item"]["call_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let name = evt["item"]["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index);
                state.current_tool_index = state.current_tool_index.max(index + 1);
                state.tool_calls.insert(index, (id.clone(), name.clone()));
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::ToolCallStart { id, name, index }])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.function_call_arguments.delta" => {
            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
                && !delta.is_empty()
            {
                let index =
                    responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::ToolCallArgumentsDelta {
                    index,
                    delta: delta.to_string(),
                }])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.function_call_arguments.done" => {
            let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
            if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index }])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.completed" => {
            if let Some(usage) = evt
                .get("response")
                .and_then(|response| response.get("usage"))
            {
                let parsed = parse_openai_compat_usage(usage);
                state.input_tokens = parsed.input_tokens;
                state.output_tokens = parsed.output_tokens;
                state.cache_read_tokens = parsed.cache_read_tokens;
                state.cache_write_tokens = parsed.cache_write_tokens;
            }
            SseLineResult::Done
        },
        "error" | "response.failed" => {
            let msg = evt["error"]["message"]
                .as_str()
                .or_else(|| evt["response"]["error"]["message"].as_str())
                .or_else(|| evt["message"].as_str())
                .unwrap_or("unknown error");
            SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::Error(msg.to_string())])
        },
        _ => SseLineResult::Skip,
    }
}

/// Generate the final events when a Responses API stream ends.
///
/// Emits `ToolCallComplete` for any pending tool calls and a final `Done` with
/// accumulated usage.
pub fn finalize_responses_stream(state: &mut ResponsesStreamState) -> Vec<StreamEvent> {
    let mut events = Vec::new();

    let mut pending: Vec<usize> = state.tool_calls.keys().copied().collect();
    pending.sort_unstable();
    for index in pending {
        if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
            events.push(StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index });
        }
    }

    events.push(StreamEvent::Done(Usage {
        input_tokens: state.input_tokens,
        output_tokens: state.output_tokens,
        cache_read_tokens: state.cache_read_tokens,
        cache_write_tokens: state.cache_write_tokens,
    }));

    events
}

/// Parse a non-streaming Responses API JSON response into [`CompletionResponse`].
///
/// The Responses API returns an `output` array containing `message` items
/// (with `content[].text`) and `function_call` items (with `call_id`, `name`,
/// `arguments`).
pub fn parse_responses_completion(resp: &serde_json::Value) -> CompletionResponse {
    let mut text: Option<String> = None;
    let mut tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall> = Vec::new();

    if let Some(output) = resp.get("output").and_then(|o| o.as_array()) {
        for item in output {
            match item["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("") {
                "message" => {
                    if let Some(content) = item.get("content").and_then(|c| c.as_array()) {
                        for part in content {
                            if part["type"].as_str() == Some("output_text")
                                && let Some(t) = part["text"].as_str()
                            {
                                text = Some(text.map_or_else(|| t.to_string(), |prev| prev + t));
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },
                "function_call" => {
                    let id = item["call_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                    let name = item["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                    let args_str = item["arguments"].as_str().unwrap_or("{}");
                    let arguments = serde_json::from_str(args_str).unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
                    tool_calls.push(ToolCall {
                        id,
                        name,
                        arguments,
                    });
                },
                _ => {},
            }
        }
    }

    let usage = resp
        .get("usage")
        .map(parse_openai_compat_usage)
        .unwrap_or_default();

    CompletionResponse {
        text,
        tool_calls,
        usage,
    }
}

// ============================================================================
// Responses API helpers (shared by openai.rs and github_copilot.rs)
// ============================================================================

/// Split system messages into `instructions` and convert the rest to Responses
/// API `input` items.
///
/// The Responses API uses a top-level `instructions` field instead of a system
/// message role.  This function extracts all system messages, joins them with
/// `\n\n`, and converts the remaining messages via [`to_responses_input`].
#[must_use]
pub fn split_responses_instructions_and_input(
    messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
) -> (Option<String>, Vec<serde_json::Value>) {
    let mut instruction_parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
    let mut non_system: Vec<ChatMessage> = Vec::new();

    for message in messages {
        match message {
            ChatMessage::System { content } => {
                if !content.trim().is_empty() {
                    instruction_parts.push(content);
                }
            },
            other => non_system.push(other),
        }
    }

    let instructions = if instruction_parts.is_empty() {
        None
    } else {
        Some(instruction_parts.join("\n\n"))
    };

    (instructions, to_responses_input(&non_system))
}

/// Resolve the output index from a Responses API event.
///
/// The Responses API uses `output_index` for items and `index` for
/// sub-item fields.  WebSocket events may also use `item_index`.
/// Falls back to `fallback` if none of these keys are present.
pub fn responses_output_index(event: &serde_json::Value, fallback: usize) -> usize {
    event
        .get("output_index")
        .or_else(|| event.get("item_index"))
        .or_else(|| event.get("index"))
        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64)
        .map(|i| i as usize)
        .unwrap_or(fallback)
}

/// State for tracking Responses API SSE streaming.
#[derive(Default)]
pub struct ResponsesStreamState {
    /// Map from index -> (call_id, name)
    pub tool_calls: HashMap<usize, (String, String)>,
    /// Set of tool call indices that have already emitted `ToolCallComplete`.
    pub completed_tool_calls: HashSet<usize>,
    /// The next tool call index to assign.
    pub current_tool_index: usize,
    pub input_tokens: u32,
    pub output_tokens: u32,
    pub cache_read_tokens: u32,
    pub cache_write_tokens: u32,
}

/// Process a single SSE data line from a Responses API stream.
///
/// Returns [`SseLineResult`] indicating whether to skip, yield events, or stop.
///
/// Handles the event types emitted by the Responses API:
/// - `response.output_text.delta` → text delta
/// - `response.output_item.added` (type=function_call) → tool call start
/// - `response.function_call_arguments.delta` → tool call arguments delta
/// - `response.function_call_arguments.done` → tool call complete
/// - `response.completed` → parse usage, done
/// - `error` / `response.failed` → error
pub fn process_responses_sse_line(data: &str, state: &mut ResponsesStreamState) -> SseLineResult {
    if data == "[DONE]" {
        return SseLineResult::Done;
    }

    let Ok(evt) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(data) else {
        return SseLineResult::Skip;
    };

    match evt["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("") {
        "response.output_text.delta" => {
            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
                && !delta.is_empty()
            {
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::Delta(delta.to_string())])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.output_item.added" => {
            if evt["item"]["type"].as_str() == Some("function_call") {
                let id = evt["item"]["call_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let name = evt["item"]["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index);
                state.current_tool_index = state.current_tool_index.max(index + 1);
                state.tool_calls.insert(index, (id.clone(), name.clone()));
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::ToolCallStart { id, name, index }])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.function_call_arguments.delta" => {
            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
                && !delta.is_empty()
            {
                let index =
                    responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::ToolCallArgumentsDelta {
                    index,
                    delta: delta.to_string(),
                }])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.function_call_arguments.done" => {
            let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
            if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
                SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index }])
            } else {
                SseLineResult::Skip
            }
        },
        "response.completed" => {
            if let Some(usage) = evt
                .get("response")
                .and_then(|response| response.get("usage"))
            {
                let parsed = parse_openai_compat_usage(usage);
                state.input_tokens = parsed.input_tokens;
                state.output_tokens = parsed.output_tokens;
                state.cache_read_tokens = parsed.cache_read_tokens;
                state.cache_write_tokens = parsed.cache_write_tokens;
            }
            SseLineResult::Done
        },
        "error" | "response.failed" => {
            let msg = evt["error"]["message"]
                .as_str()
                .or_else(|| evt["response"]["error"]["message"].as_str())
                .or_else(|| evt["message"].as_str())
                .unwrap_or("unknown error");
            SseLineResult::Events(vec![StreamEvent::Error(msg.to_string())])
        },
        _ => SseLineResult::Skip,
    }
}

/// Generate the final events when a Responses API stream ends.
///
/// Emits `ToolCallComplete` for any pending tool calls and a final `Done` with
/// accumulated usage.
pub fn finalize_responses_stream(state: &mut ResponsesStreamState) -> Vec<StreamEvent> {
    let mut events = Vec::new();

    let mut pending: Vec<usize> = state.tool_calls.keys().copied().collect();
    pending.sort_unstable();
    for index in pending {
        if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
            events.push(StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index });
        }
    }

    events.push(StreamEvent::Done(Usage {
        input_tokens: state.input_tokens,
        output_tokens: state.output_tokens,
        cache_read_tokens: state.cache_read_tokens,
        cache_write_tokens: state.cache_write_tokens,
    }));

    events
}

/// Parse a non-streaming Responses API JSON response into [`CompletionResponse`].
///
/// The Responses API returns an `output` array containing `message` items
/// (with `content[].text`) and `function_call` items (with `call_id`, `name`,
/// `arguments`).
pub fn parse_responses_completion(resp: &serde_json::Value) -> CompletionResponse {
    let mut text: Option<String> = None;
    let mut tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall> = Vec::new();

    if let Some(output) = resp.get("output").and_then(|o| o.as_array()) {
        for item in output {
            match item["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("") {
                "message" => {
                    if let Some(content) = item.get("content").and_then(|c| c.as_array()) {
                        for part in content {
                            if part["type"].as_str() == Some("output_text")
                                && let Some(t) = part["text"].as_str()
                            {
                                text = Some(text.map_or_else(|| t.to_string(), |prev| prev + t));
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },
                "function_call" => {
                    let id = item["call_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                    let name = item["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
                    let args_str = item["arguments"].as_str().unwrap_or("{}");
                    let arguments = serde_json::from_str(args_str).unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
                    tool_calls.push(ToolCall {
                        id,
                        name,
                        arguments,
                    });
                },
                _ => {},
            }
        }
    }

    let usage = resp
        .get("usage")
        .map(parse_openai_compat_usage)
        .unwrap_or_default();

    CompletionResponse {
        text,
        tool_calls,
        usage,
    }
}

        state.tool_calls.insert(0, ("call_1".into(), "test".into()));
        state
            .tool_calls
            .insert(1, ("call_2".into(), "test2".into()));
        state.completed_tool_calls.insert(0); // only 0 is completed
        state.input_tokens = 30;
        state.output_tokens = 10;

        let events = finalize_responses_stream(&mut state);
        // Should emit ToolCallComplete for index 1 (pending) + Done
        assert_eq!(events.len(), 2);
        assert!(matches!(&events[0], StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete {
            index: 1
        }));
        assert!(matches!(
            &events[1],
            StreamEvent::Done(usage) if usage.input_tokens == 30 && usage.output_tokens == 10
        ));
    }

        let mut state = ResponsesStreamState {

        assert_eq!(events.len(), 1);
        assert!(matches!(
            &events[0],
            StreamEvent::Done(usage) if usage.input_tokens == 10 && usage.output_tokens == 5
        ));
    }
